heading: Acquiring land for park, parkway, or playground purposes
label: 8731 Acquiring land for park, parkway, or playground purposes
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)8731(a)40:72 (1st, 4th sentences)., formerly § 2, ; renumbered § 11, (1st sentence), .June 6, 1924, ch. 270, § 1143 Stat. 463July 19, 1952, ch. 949, § 266 Stat. 7918731(b)40:72 (2d, 3d sentence).8731(c)40:73 (3d–last sentences).(3d–last sentences), formerly § 3 (3d–last sentences), ; renumbered § 12, (1st sentence), .June 6, 1924, ch. 270, § 1243 Stat. 463July 19, 1952, ch. 949, § 266 Stat. 7918731(d)40:72 (last sentence).In subsection (a), the words “or a majority thereof” are omitted as unnecessary.In subsection (b)(2), the words “ (, )” are substituted for 40:72 (2d sentence words after “in accordance with”) because provisions in (ch. 837, ), established the act as permanent and general. The act therefore was classified to 40:120, which was superseded by the Act of (ch. 416, ), which was classified to 40:361 et seq. That law was repealed by (, ), with the subject matter of those sections being restated in section 1 of that Act.section 1 of the Act of December 23, 1963Public Law 88–24177 Stat. 572section 3 of the Act of August 30, 189026 Stat. 41245 Stat. 1415section 21(b) of the Act of December 23, 1963Public Law 88–24177 Stat. 627March 1, 1929In subsection (b)(3), the words “either by purchase or condemnation proceedings” and “as to acquisition and payment for the lands as it shall determine upon” are omitted as unnecessary.In subsection (c)(1), the words “Director of the National Park Service” are substituted for “Chief of Engineers of the United States Army” [subsequently changed to “Director of Public Buildings and Public Parks” by (ch. 339, )] because of section 1(words before 3d comma in 2d complete par. on p. 389) of the Act of (ch. 38, ).section 3 of the Act of February 26, 192543 Stat. 98348 Stat. 389March 2, 1934In subsection (c)(2), the words “in Maryland or Virginia” are substituted for “outside the District of Columbia” for clarity and for consistency in this section. The words “such agreements to be subject to the approval of the President” are omitted because of 40:72 (last sentence), restated as subsection (d).

children.heading: .—Authority To Acquire Land
children.label: a .—Authority To Acquire Land
children.text: The National Capitol Planning Commission shall acquire land the Planning Commission believes is necessary and desirable in the District of Columbia and adjacent areas in Maryland and Virginia for suitable development of the National Capital park, parkway, and playground system. The acquisition must be within the limits of the appropriations made for those purposes. The Planning Commission shall request the advice of the Commission of Fine Arts in selecting land to be acquired.

children.heading: How Land May Be Acquired.—
children.label: b How Land May Be Acquired.—

children.heading: .—Purchase or condemnation proceeding
children.label: 1 .—Purchase or condemnation proceeding
children.text: The National Capital Planning Commission may buy land when the land can be acquired at a price the Planning Commission considers reasonable or by a condemnation proceeding when the land cannot be bought at a reasonable price.

children.heading: .—Land in the district of columbia
children.label: 2 .—Land in the district of columbia
children.text: A condemnation proceeding to acquire land in the District of Columbia shall be conducted in accordance with (, ).section 1 of the Act of December 23, 1963Public Law 88–24177 Stat. 571

children.heading: .—Land in maryland or virginia
children.label: 3 .—Land in maryland or virginia
children.text: The Planning Commission may acquire land in Maryland or Virginia under arrangements agreed to by the Commission and the proper officials of Maryland or Virginia.

children.heading: .—Land in the district of columbia
children.label: 1 .—Land in the district of columbia
children.text: Land acquired in the District of Columbia shall be a part of the park system of the District of Columbia and be under the control of the Director of the National Park Service. The National Capital Planning Commission may assign areas suitable for playground purposes to the control of the Mayor of the District of Columbia for playground purposes.

children.heading: .—Land in maryland or virginia
children.label: 2 .—Land in maryland or virginia
children.text: Land acquired in Maryland or Virginia shall be controlled as determined by agreement between the Planning Commission and the proper officials of Maryland or Virginia.

children.heading: .—Presidential Approval Required
children.label: d .—Presidential Approval Required
children.text: The designation of all land to be acquired by condemnation, all contracts to purchase land, and all agreements between the National Capital Planning Commission and the officials of Maryland and Virginia are subject to the approval of the President.
